hooks.//: L-1 hardening: jq -r returns 'null' on absence; awk filters strict basename pattern; while read -r preserves whitespace; printf %q quotes any shell-meta on display. Hook is decorative — never invokes external tools — but defensive against malformed inputs.
hooks.command: jq -r '(.tool_input.file_path // "")' | awk '/\/findings\/T[0-9]+\.md$/ && !/[`$;&|<>(){}*?~!]/ {print}' | while IFS= read -r f; do [ -n "$f" ] && printf '[hook] findings written: %q (validator will be invoked by the worker)\n' "$f"; done
